skills.name: agent-architect
skills.description: Analyze a coding project and design a tailored AI development environment: recommend agents, skills, instruction files, MCP servers, and workflow composition across Claude Code and Codex. Also audit an existing AI setup for gaps, overlap, or bloat. Use when setting up, overhauling, or auditing a project's AI tooling as a whole, not for individual agent edits or skill management.

skills.name: agent-lifecycle-manager
skills.description: Manage the lifecycle of sub-agents: create or update agent packages, validate cross-surface agent contracts, evaluate routing behavior, tighten invocation boundaries, tune Codex runtime defaults, install or publish agents, or audit an agent library. Use only when the request is explicitly about agents or agent libraries, not for ordinary coding, implementation, or general project planning.

skills.name: agentic-development
skills.description: Manual-first workflow for designing, building, evaluating, and upgrading real agentic systems. Use when the job involves tool-using agents, agent orchestration, agent runtimes or platforms, multi-agent systems, framework selection, evals, or guardrails; do not use for prompt-only work or ordinary implementation planning.

skills.name: ask-danker
skills.description: 本仓库 skills 的路由器——按你当前的处境指路：该用哪个 skill、走哪条流、下一步交给谁。手动调用（/ask-danker），模型不会自动触发。

skills.name: blind-spot-pass
skills.description: 开工前对陌生区域做盲区侦察：从代码库考古（git 历史、相似实现、隐形约定、危险区、邻接面）挖出你没意识到要问的 unknown unknowns，产出带证据的盲区清单、改写后的更好 prompt 和 reference 清单。Use before long tasks in unfamiliar territory to find unknown unknowns.

skills.name: brainstorming
skills.description: Turn ambiguous ideas or competing directions into one approved decision before planning or implementation. Invoke explicitly, or via routing from other skills when direction is still unchosen.

skills.name: business-plan
skills.description: Structured business planning and business-analysis workflow for business plans, investor-ready financial analysis, market sizing, pitch deck narrative, strategic review, and valuation. Best for substantive commercial deliverables rather than open-ended ideation, pure research, or final file production.

skills.name: clarify
skills.description: Turn ambiguous implementation requests into actionable scope before execution. Use when asked to clarify requirements, disambiguate a request, or spec a change before building. Auto-activate only when an implementation request contains direct contradictions, is missing acceptance criteria that would materially change the build, or asks to proceed on documented assumptions. Not for direction-finding or brainstorming, nor for already-specific actionable tasks.

skills.name: codeagent
skills.description: Execute codeagent-wrapper for multi-backend AI code tasks with Codex, Claude, and Gemini backends, file references, structured output, and parallel task execution.

skills.name: control-plane-auditor
skills.description: Audit a repository's control plane health across seven layers (memory, invariant, protocol, permission, sensorium, evaluation, governance) and produce an actionable improvement report. Covers instruction-file completeness, rule enforcement coverage (mechanical / review-only / phantom), doc freshness, test infrastructure, and agent readiness. Explicit bootstrap/repair write modes turn audit gaps into the control plane itself: spec-previewed scaffolding of instruction files, command entry points, verification matrix, and guardrails, validated by guardrail self-test.

skills.name: deep-research
skills.description: Explicit, citation-backed web research for deep dives, due diligence, market analysis, and comprehensive comparisons; not for quick factual lookups or routine coding/content tasks.

skills.name: diagnosing-bugs
skills.description: 因未知失败的诊断纪律：先建红色反馈回路（10 级构建阶梯），后假设——没有一条已跑过、能红的命令就禁止读代码编理论；随后最小化复现、3-5 个可证伪假设排序、单变量插桩、带 seam 纪律的回归测试与归因；难缠 bug 可选多代理扇出模式（回路构建与假设生成并行化）。Use to diagnose or debug hard bugs and performance regressions where the cause is unknown, fixes keep bouncing back, or CI fails without a local repro.

skills.name: editorial-review
skills.description: Comprehensive editorial review for Chinese content before publication, covering typos, grammar, punctuation, fact-checking, political compliance, and advertising law.

skills.name: entropy-review
skills.description: Review a change set for entropy: naming drift, pattern duplication and contagion, error-model and state-model splits, doc staleness. Consistency and drift only, not correctness. Invoke explicitly or via routing from review.

skills.name: future-aware-architecture
skills.description: Manual-first architecture methodology for system design and technology selection using problem-first framing, stack-agnostic trade-offs, uncertainty handling, future-aware decision lenses, and context-aware grounding.

skills.name: gh-create-issue
skills.description: Create GitHub issues from PRDs, requirements, or feature descriptions, choosing between a single issue and an epic plus sub-issues using gh CLI labels and dependencies.

skills.name: git-worktree-workflows
skills.description: Guide safe git worktree workflows for explicit worktree tasks, including inspect, compare, cleanup, and repair, plus high-confidence parallel checkout requests while deferring repo bootstrap and broader advanced-Git sessions where worktrees are incidental.

skills.name: grill-me
skills.description: 对已有 plan/design 做对抗式压测：沿决策树逐分支追问，一次只问一个问题且每问附推荐答案，能从 codebase 查到就不问用户，直到达成共同理解。Use to stress-test a plan or get grilled on a design before implementation.

skills.name: grill-with-docs
skills.description: grill-me 的领域增强变体：对抗式压测计划/设计的同时对齐项目术语、用具体场景探边界、与代码交叉核对，并把术语 inline 沉淀到 openspec/glossary.md、把够格的长期决策落到 docs/adr/。Use to stress-test a plan against the project's domain language and persist glossary terms and ADRs inline.

skills.name: handoff
skills.description: 把当前会话压缩成一份交接文档，让新会话或另一个 agent 无缝接续：会话独有状态（决策、工作流计数器、已排除路径、下一步）写入文档，已沉淀的工件只引用不复制。手动调用（/handoff），适配 issue 工作流的跨会话续跑。

skills.name: hook-development
skills.description: Cross-platform guidance for designing, configuring, debugging, and validating Claude Code and Codex hooks. Use for agent hook events, configuration, scripts, matchers, exit codes, and dual-platform setups; not for React hooks, webhooks, or GitHub Actions.

skills.name: implementation-planning
skills.description: Deep executable planning for complex implementation work — phases, dependencies, verification, rollback — after the direction is chosen. Invoke explicitly: by the user, the planner agent, or stage-change-pipeline.

skills.name: improve-codebase-architecture
skills.description: 基于“深模块”哲学寻找架构改进点：用 explorer subagent 扫描 codebase，对照 openspec/glossary.md 术语与 docs/adr/ 决策，找出 shallow modules 并提出 deepening 重构（提升 testability 与 AI 可导航性），产出可视化 HTML 评审并进入 grill 对话逐个落地；落定的 deepening 可经 gh-create-issue / stage-change-pipeline 变成可追踪工作项。Use to improve architecture, find refactoring opportunities, consolidate tightly-coupled modules, make a codebase more testable, or turn a settled deepening into trackable issues.

skills.name: meta-loop
skills.description: Judge or design any produce → check → fix → re-check loop against a 10-dimension rubric (verification, review, evaluator-optimizer, agent self-review). Invoke explicitly, or when another skill names it for a loop audit.

skills.name: prd-authoring
skills.description: Write a review-ready PRD — prioritized requirements, testable acceptance criteria, success metrics — from validated discovery output once a direction is already chosen. Triggers: "write a PRD", "product spec", "requirements doc", "写PRD", "需求文档", "产品规格". Not for open-ended ideation (brainstorming), requirement disambiguation (clarify), fresh cited research (deep-research), business-model work (business-plan), or execution planning (implementation-planning).

skills.name: project-documentation
skills.description: Govern a repository's docs/: restructure, consolidate overlapping pages, refresh stale docs after code changes, audit drift and dead links. Invoke explicitly — by the user or stage-change-pipeline. Not for READMEs or PR review.

skills.name: project-instruction-bootstrap
skills.description: 为目标项目安装 pack 之后，扫描项目并补全/对齐它自己的 CLAUDE.md 与 AGENTS.md。推荐生成模式：项目维护 instructions/agents/{shared,claude,codex}.md 源，本 skill 充当生成器拼接两文件（shared 段单一事实源、零漂移），不给项目装脚本或 hook；已有手写指令则走增量兼容模式。写入项目自身约定（技术栈/命令/目录）、已装 pack/skill/agent 的使用与触发约定，以及可移植的编排骨架（Observable Completion、反熵下沉、按模型纠偏、项目本地适配指引）。增量补全、绝不覆盖已有内容、写前出 diff。Use after installing a pack into a target project to bootstrap or align its CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md.

skills.name: prompt-engineering
skills.description: Manual-first workflow for redesigning, debugging, hardening, and productionizing LLM prompts or system prompts across models. Use when the job needs end-to-end prompt work, not quick technique lookup.

skills.name: readme-craftsman
skills.description: Create, update, or review a repository README when the user explicitly asks for README work. Use it to draft a new README, refresh an existing README after project changes, or audit a README against the current repository. Do not use it for general documentation, API docs, architecture docs, or documentation tasks that do not specifically target a README file. Not for trivial single-line README edits such as fixing a typo — just make the edit directly.

skills.name: repo-entropy-audit
skills.description: Scan an entire repository for entropy across six axes (structure, semantics, behavior, context, protocol, control) and produce a module-level heatmap, trend comparison against baseline, and prioritized cleanup targets. Use for periodic health checks or before major releases.

skills.name: research-engineering-handoff
skills.description: Convert an approved research decision into a bounded engineering handoff preserving scientific intent, invariants, evidence oracles, and human decision authority. Invoke explicitly, typically from research-lifecycle before stage-change-pipeline.

skills.name: research-lifecycle
skills.description: Research control-plane router: frames a phenomenon, capability gap, or decision need, then orchestrates question framing, study design, evidence synthesis, traceability, and engineering handoff. Invoke explicitly for substantive scientific work.

skills.name: research-profile-bootstrap
skills.description: Create or update the lean project-local research profile at research/project-profile.md by scanning repository evidence: scientific objects, data/model surfaces, invariants, study practices, human gates. Use when installing research-engineering or when a project gains a recurring scientific surface. Not for per-study protocols or the software-risk profile at openspec/project-profile.md.

skills.name: research-question-framing
skills.description: Turn a phenomenon, scientific uncertainty, capability gap, method idea, data problem, or operational decision into a bounded research contract — objects, intended claims, alternatives, evidence needs, scope, risks, downstream route. Use before study design when the problem is not yet decision-ready. Not for hypothesis-only templating or ordinary implementation requirements.

skills.name: review
skills.description: Structured review of concrete artifacts: PRs, diffs, commits, branches, staged changes, named files, docs, API specs, database migrations, or infrastructure config. Activate on explicit, artifact-scoped review requests — "code review", "review my changes", "find bugs in", "security review". Produces severity-graded findings with fix directions. Do NOT activate for brainstorming, open-ended design feedback, governance/library audits, or whether-an-approach-seems-good conversations.

skills.name: risk-adaptive-cross-review
skills.description: Multi-perspective, risk-adaptive review for PRs, diffs, branches, OpenSpec changes, or stage artifacts — reviewers split across correctness, integration, security/performance, test evidence, spec compliance, or invariant/state-machine compatibility. Use on explicit asks for risk-adaptive, multi-perspective, parallel, cross-review, or high-risk PR review. Not for ordinary quick review.

skills.name: scientific-evidence-synthesis
skills.description: Synthesize completed study evidence into a claim-evidence matrix separating support, counter-evidence, uncertainty, and unsupported conclusions. Invoked by research-lifecycle after study execution, or explicitly by the user.

skills.name: skill-lifecycle-manager
skills.description: Manage the lifecycle of agent skills: discover patterns, create or update a skill, validate it, evaluate it, project or install it, or audit a skill library. Use only when the request is explicitly about skills or skill libraries, not for general code tasks or agent management.

skills.name: skill-researcher
skills.description: Research existing community skills for a skill topic, pause for candidate confirmation, and produce a Fusion Report for downstream skill creation.

skills.name: stage-change-pipeline
skills.description: Turn a development-stage design package into a reviewed OpenSpec change and fine-grained GitHub issues with clear module boundaries and small-PR execution scope.

skills.name: study-design
skills.description: Design a reviewable scientific study protocol: units, baselines, controls, observables, uncertainty, evidence oracles, stop gates, and protocol freeze. Invoked by research-lifecycle after framing, or explicitly by the user.

skills.name: subagent-workflow
skills.description: End-to-end GitHub issue implementation workflow orchestrated by Claude Code or Codex, delegating implementation, cross-review, and finding verification to subagents (implementer, reviewer, verifier). Covers OpenSpec fixtures, risk-adaptive review, verification, PR evidence, CI, and human-gated merge.

skills.name: theory-to-code-traceability
skills.description: Build or review a traceability contract from scientific assumptions, equations, units, and invariants to code surfaces and verification cases. Invoke explicitly, typically from research-lifecycle for scientific model changes.

agents.name: debugger
agents.description: Systematic debugging agent that diagnoses bugs, test failures, and unexpected behavior through hypothesis-driven investigation.

agents.name: docs-researcher
agents.description: Documentation-backed research agent for verifying APIs, SDKs, frameworks, and libraries against official sources.

agents.name: explorer
agents.description: Use when a task needs fast read-only codebase mapping, evidence gathering, impact analysis, or file and symbol discovery before implementation or review.

agents.name: implementer
agents.description: Code implementation agent that writes, modifies, and refactors code based on plans, specs, or direct instructions.

agents.name: issue-scribe
agents.description: Follow-up capture agent: takes a raw observation noticed during primary work, verifies it read-only, determines origin, boundary, and solution direction, dedups against existing issues, and files one structured GitHub issue for later delivery. Never fixes anything itself.

agents.name: monitor
agents.description: Cheap-model watchdog for harness-external long-running jobs (slurm, remote PID over ssh, CI runs). ID-based completion detection, one blocking poll loop per check, quiet waits, final-state report with evidence. Read-only; never does side work.

agents.name: planner
agents.description: Architecture and implementation planning agent that designs step-by-step plans with trade-off analysis and escalates to implementation-planning for complex technical execution plans.

agents.name: researcher
agents.description: Web research agent for deep investigation, multi-source verification, and structured report generation.

agents.name: reviewer
agents.description: Code review agent that performs structured, severity-graded reviews on PRs, diffs, and code changes.

agents.name: verifier
agents.description: Independent finding-verification agent. Adjudicates one failure-class batch of candidate review findings (at most 5) with exactly one verdict per candidate — CONFIRMED, PLAUSIBLE, or REFUTED — using only diff/spec/code/test evidence. Read-only; never a reviewer that produced any candidate in its batch.

hooks.name: large-file-guard
hooks.description: PreToolUse hook that blocks `git commit` when the commit includes text files over a line-count threshold (default 1000). Incremental ratchet: only files touched by the commit are checked; legacy large files do not block until modified. Configurable via .large-file-guard.json.

hooks.name: review-gate
hooks.description: PreToolUse hook that denies implementer/reviewer subagent spawns while the subagent-workflow three-round hard gate is locked. No-op until the project has a .review-gate.json state file (maintained by the skill's review_gate.py CLI), so it is safe to install everywhere.

hooks.name: worktree-guard
hooks.description: PreToolUse hook that blocks file writes outside declared worktree roots. No-op until the project declares .worktree-guard.json, so it is safe to install everywhere.

packs.name: agentic-issue-delivery
packs.description: Workflow pack for turning design-stage work into implementation-ready issues, then executing those issues through subagent-driven review and delivery gates, with whole-repo entropy governance.

packs.name: codebase-stewardship
packs.description: 周期性/按需的代码健康治理：深化模块、治乱删冗、定架构方向，共用 grill 对抗式对话与 openspec/glossary.md + docs/adr/ 领域沉淀。区别于 agentic-issue-delivery 的一次性 issue 交付流水线。

packs.name: product-manager
packs.description: Product strategy, requirements, and research workflow pack.

packs.name: research-engineering
packs.description: Research control-plane pack for turning phenomena, capability gaps, scientific ideas, model-process changes, data/method needs, and operational questions into governed research artifacts, reviewed evidence, human decisions, and optional engineering handoffs without constraining work to a closed task taxonomy.
